description: International outlaw motorcycle club founded in Montreal, Canada in 1986 by Salvatore Cazzetta and his brother Giovanni. It competed with the Hells Angels during the Quebec Biker War (1994-2002), resulting in over 160 deaths. The original club was patched over into the Bandidos in 2001, but a second version was revived in 2008. As of 2022, it has over 120 chapters worldwide.

country_links.evidence_quote: including the United States, Australia, Germany, Russia, Belgium, New Zealand, Sweden, Norway, France, South Africa, England, Spain, Georgia, Hong Kong, Armenia, Indonesia, Thailand, Vietnam, Philippines
country_links.context: The Rock Machine has spread to Armenia.

country_links.evidence_quote: The claims of the Rock Machine to have a chapter in Daytona Beach, Florida seems to have no foundation in reality. ... The Quebec biker war had made the name Rock Machine well known and a chapter had founded in Australia.
country_links.context: The Rock Machine has a chapter in Australia and is involved in a conflict with the Rebels Motorcycle Club there.
